results.judge_reason: Pure source-domain heraldry article; no LLM/representation content whatsoever.
Generic source-domain guidelines snippet, no overlap with LLM application.
Source-domain historical analysis only; lacks LLM-side mechanism.
Constraint propagation for LLM CSP solving, not depth-adjacency channel alternation.
Vision-language latent connector preserving semantics; unrelated to layer-adjacency alternation regularizer.
Adversarial preference alignment, no representation-contrast across layers.
Generic ICLR alignment context; snippet too sparse to evidence mechanism overlap.
Visual-token reduction under cross-modal misalignment, unrelated to channel-class alternation across depth.

verdict: PASS (mechanical) but UNSAFE: queries failed to retrieve the primary suspect literature. The depth-adjacency channel-class alternation idea has substantive overlap with (a) batch-norm/layer-norm alternation and Pre-LN vs Post-LN ordering studies, (b) attention-pattern cycle/period literature (e.g., alternating attention heads, periodic depth structure), and (c) channel-wise normalization alternation studies. Confirm-bias risk: source-side queries hit only heraldry; LLM-side query found only superficial constraint/alignment papers. Recommend secondary search for 'layer-norm alternation transformer depth', 'attention cycle periodicity layers', 'channel norm class alternation regularizer' before treating as novel.
comment: Primary suspects PASS hides substantive coverage in batch-norm/layer-norm alternation and attention-cycle literature that was not retrieved by the two queries; mechanical PASS should not be read as substantive novelty.
